summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on BZA Case 2023-011, a variance request by Michael Shepard to allow a detached accessory garage built zero feet from the public right-of-way at his property on Clairville Drive. Township zoning staff recommended denial because the structure was built without a permit, extends into the public right-of-way, and the applicant had not met the conditions of a prior 2021 variance resolution including lot consolidation. The board unanimously denied the variance and advised the applicant to reapply for the original variance process, consolidate his lots, and move the structure to comply with required setbacks.
decisions: Denied variance request BZA 2023-011 for zero-foot setback from public right-of-way for detached accessory garage (unanimous)
action_items: Michael Shepard to resubmit variance application including the new garage structure and lot consolidation through the zoning process
Michael Shepard to move the accessory garage structure to comply with R1 district setback requirements (10 feet from principal structure and behind the front facade setback line)
Michael Shepard to complete lot consolidation through township and county processes
Zoning staff to assist Michael Shepard through the resubmission and variance process
key_amounts: $1,600 paid by applicant for original permit applications and fees in 2021
50-foot minimum setback from street right-of-way required in R1 district
48-foot front yard setback previously approved for the home along State Route 48
15-foot side yard setback previously approved via 2021 variance
10-foot minimum distance required between accessory structure and principal building
7 feet current distance between garage and principal structure (3 feet less than required)
9 feet from physical road to structure per applicant's site plan
notable_topics: Variance request for detached accessory garage built zero feet from public right-of-way (BZA 2023-011)
Structure built without zoning permit or building permit
Failure to meet conditions of prior 2021 variance resolution (R-20-2021)
Lot consolidation requirement not completed from prior resolution
Prior resolution deemed expired/null and void due to unmet conditions
Property maintenance complaint that initiated the current proceedings
Applicant's claim that paperwork was lost by previous zoning staff
Structure potentially extending beyond property line into public right-of-way
notes: This is a Board of Zoning Appeals (BZA) meeting, not a Board of Trustees meeting. The applicant built a metal garage without permits and it appears to extend past his property line into the public right-of-way. A prior 2021 variance resolution (R-20-2021) had granted reduced setbacks with conditions including lot consolidation within six months, but those conditions were never fulfilled, rendering the resolution expired. The board advised the applicant to start the variance process over, including the new garage, and to move the structure to comply with setbacks before or as part of that process. The applicant claimed confusion due to prior zoning staff losing paperwork. Next meeting scheduled for November 20, 2023. Meeting adjourned at 7:21 PM.
title: BZA 10-16-23
